fields.name: Worksheet Template Name
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the name of the planning worksheet template.
The program automatically retrieves the name from the Name field in the Planning Wksh. Template table, but you can change it.

fields.name: Worksheet Batch Name
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the name of the planning worksheet batch.
The program automatically retrieves the name from the Name field in the Planning Wksh. Batch table, but you can change it.

fields.name: Worksheet Line No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the requisition worksheet line number of the planning routing line.
The program automatically retrieves the number from the Line No. field in the Requisition Line table.

fields.name: Operation No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the operation number for this planning routing line.

fields.name: Next Operation No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the next operation number if you use parallel routings.

fields.name: Previous Operation No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the previous operation number and shows the operation that is run directly before the operation.

fields.name: Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of operation.

fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the involved entry or record, according to the specified number series.

fields.name: Work Center No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a work center number.
If the capacity carrying out an operation is a work center, the value is copied from the No. field. If it is a machine, the content of the field on the machine card is copied from the Work Center No. field.

fields.name: Work Center Group Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code copied from the Work Center card. To change the contents of the field, click the field and select a different work center group number from the Work Center Groups window.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a description of the task related to this routing line.

fields.name: Setup Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the setup time using the unit of measure from the Setup Time Unit of Measure field on the work or machine center card.

fields.name: Run Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the run time of the operation.

fields.name: Wait Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the wait time.

fields.name: Move Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the move time using the unit of measure in the Move Time Unit of Measure field on the machine or work center card.

fields.name: Fixed Scrap Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies a fixed scrap quantity for this routing line.

fields.name: Lot Size
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of items that are included in the same operation at the same time. The run time on routing lines is reduced proportionally to the lot size. For example, if the lot size is two pieces, the run time will be reduced by half.

fields.name: Scrap Factor %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the scrap factor as a percentage.

fields.name: Setup Time Unit of Meas. Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the unit of measure code for measuring setup time to use when you manufacture the item. click the field to see a list of available unit of measure codes.
The program automatically fills in this field when you enter the number of a machine or work center on the line, but you can change it.
Setup time is the time required for setting up the machine center or work center to go from production of the last item in one lot to the first item of the next lot.
Note that the throughput time can be expressed in various units of measure. However, if you do so it will affect cost calculations.

fields.name: Run Time Unit of Meas. Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the unit of measure code for the run time to use when you manufacture the item. click the field to see a list of available unit of measure codes.
The program automatically fills in this field when you enter the number of a machine or work center on the line, but you can change it.
Run time is the time required to process or manufacture an item or a lot in a specific operation. Run time does not include setup time.
Note that the throughput time can be expressed in various units of measure. However, if you do so it will affect cost calculations.

fields.name: Wait Time Unit of Meas. Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the unit of measure code for the wait time to use when you manufacture the item. click the field to see a list of available unit of measure codes.
The program automatically fills in this field when you enter the number of a machine or work center on the line, but you can change it.
Wait time is the time that items in process must wait to be moved to the next machine or work center. In other words, it is the time between steps in the manufacturing process.
Note that the throughput time can be expressed in various units of measure. However, if you do so it will affect cost calculations.

fields.name: Move Time Unit of Meas. Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the unit of measure code for the move time to use when you manufacture the item. click the field to see a list of available unit of measure codes.
The program automatically fills in this field when you enter the number of a machine or work center on the line, but you can change it.
Move time is the time a lot or batch of an order spends in transit from one operation to another.
Note that the throughput time can be expressed in various units of measure. However, if you do so it will affect cost calculations.

fields.name: Description 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies additional description text.

fields.name: Minimum Process Time
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a minimum process time.
The program automatically fills in this field when you enter the number of a machine or work center on the line, but you can change it.
The minimum process time is the minimum time required for an operation. This field provides additional information about the process.

fields.name: Maximum Process Time
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a maximum process time.
The program automatically fills in this field when you enter the number of a machine or work center on the line, but you can change it.
The maximum process time is the maximum time required for an operation. This field provides additional information about the process.

fields.name: Concurrent Capacities
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of machines or personnel that can perform their expected functions simultaneously.

fields.name: Send-Ahead Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the output of the operation that must be completed before the next operation can be started.

fields.name: Routing Link Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the routing link code.
click the field to see the available routing link codes in the Routing Link table.

fields.name: Standard Task Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a standard task code.
It is a code for frequently performed tasks. The program copies the contents of this field to the Description field on this line.
click the field to see the standard task codes in the Standard Task table.

fields.name: Unit Cost per
fields.tooltip: Specifies the unit cost for this operation if it is different than the unit cost on the work center or machine center card.

fields.name: Recalculate
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Fixed Scrap Qty. (Accum.)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the accumulated fixed scrap quantity.
Fixed scrap quantity is the quantity of scrap generated at the beginning of an operation. In other words, the first component produced in some operations is always damaged, and it can therefore not be used in the subsequent process. Thus, the Fixed Scrap Qty. (Accum.) field contains the accumulated fixed scrap quantity of all operations in the routing specified on this planning line.

fields.name: Scrap Factor % (Accumulated)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the accumulated scrap factor as a percentage.
The scrap factor defines the scrap that can only be accounted for at the end of the operation. This field accumulates the scrap factor for each operation in the routing.

fields.name: Sequence No. (Actual)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the current sequence number.
You can plan a production order using either forward or backward scheduling. Depending on the scheduling method, the program assigns sequence numbers to each operation.
The program uses this field internally.

fields.name: Direct Unit Cost
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the direct unit cost for this planning routing line.
The program automatically fills in this field when you enter the number of a machine or work center on the line, but you can change it.
The direct unit cost is used in the calculation of items such as the cost per unit of material worked on or cost per hour as defined in capacity unit of measure. The direct unit cost differs from the unit cost in that the unit cost includes the indirect cost.

fields.name: Indirect Cost %
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the indirect cost as a percentage for this planning routing line.
The program automatically fills in this field when you enter the number of a machine or work center on the line, but you can change it.
The indirect cost is costs such as general operating cost of the capacity center that is not directly incurred by a particular operation. The indirect cost is added to the direct unit cost to determine the unit cost.

fields.name: Overhead Rate
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the overhead rate for this planning routing line.
The program automatically fills in this field when you enter the number of a machine or work center on the line, but you can change it.
The overhead rate is a fixed amount. You can set up an overhead rate that covers costs other than material or capacity costs, for example transportation expenses.
The overhead rate is added to the total cost of the routing line.

fields.name: Output Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the output quantity for this planning routing line.

fields.name: Starting Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the starting time for the operation for this planning routing line.

fields.name: Starting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the starting date for the operation for this planning routing line.

fields.name: Ending Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ending time of the operation for this planning routing line.

fields.name: Ending Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ending date of the operation for this planning routing line.

fields.name: Unit Cost Calculation
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the basis for the unit cost calculation.
click the field to select one of the following options:

Time: The calculation will be based on the time spent.


Unit: The calculation will be based on produced units.

fields.name: Input Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specify a capacity need that will be added to the calculation of the expected capacity need for the planning routing line.

fields.name: Critical Path
fields.tooltip:
Specifies that this planning routing line is a critical path.
A path may be considered critical for avoiding bottlenecks in the manufacturing process, for example, and may therefore need special attention.

fields.name: Starting Date-Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the starting date and the starting time, which are combined in a format called "starting date-time".

fields.name: Ending Date-Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ending date and the ending time, which are combined in a format called "ending date-time".

fields.name: Expected Operation Cost Amt.
fields.tooltip: Automatically calculates a value based on the capacity need when a production order is refreshed or replanned.

fields.name: Expected Capacity Ovhd. Cost
fields.tooltip: Automatically calculates a value based on the capacity need when a production order is refreshed or replanned.

fields.name: Expected Capacity Need
fields.tooltip: Specifies the expected capacity need for the production order routing line on the planning line.
